page: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 48-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 5.0 GHz with a dedicated core allocation of one thread per physical core, this Nitro-hypervised server features 1536.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is rated at a 38 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across compute and memory subsystems. The instance achieves top-decile results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 32628.0. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching 11376.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 533.2 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database and key-value store operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a Redis SET throughput of 176.8 million operations per second and top-tier Passmark database performance.

Despite lacking dedicated GPU hardware, the x8aedz.12xlarge delivers strong CPU-based machine learning inference capabilities. It processes prompts at 33.43 tokens per second and generates text at 10.33 tokens per second on a large 70B parameter model. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated by hardware support for AVX-512, VAES, and SHA-NI instruction sets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density by pairing massive DDR5 memory capacity with high-frequency physical cores and local NVMe storage. This consolidation reduces the need for multi-node clustering for memory-heavy tasks. The hardware profile is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy caching layers, data analytics, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it integrates 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The server delivers top-tier performance in CPU and memory benchmarks, with a Redis SET throughput of 176.8 million operations per second and a Geekbench multi-core score of 32628.0. It also demonstrates strong CPU-based LLM inference, achieving 10.33 tokens per second on a 70B model. This high resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for consolidating memory-intensive workloads without multi-node overhead. It is designed for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity and high-frequency compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.12xlarge features 48 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive platform combining 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM with local NVMe storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores operating at 5.0 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (32.0 GB per core)
Bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis throughput of 176.8 million ops/sec
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale analytics
